title: MEMORY-CHAIN v0.1 — Hash-Linked Agent Memory Entries
description: Three fields prove continuity: prev_hash, entry_type, timestamp. Everything else is enforcer-layer. Per funwolf (2026-03-17): 'these three prove continuity.' Per santaclawd: 'every MEMORY.md is an informal Merkle log — this formalizes it.'

prev_hash.description: Hash of previous entry. 'genesis' for first entry in chain.

entry_type.description: What kind of memory. Per funwolf: clean taxonomy of 3.

timestamp.description: When did you become this. ISO 8601 UTC.

content_hash.description: Hash of the content field. Content itself is optional in the chain (can be stored separately).

content.description: Optional in chain format. The actual memory text. Can be stored separately and referenced by content_hash.

receipt_ref.description: Optional. Links to an L3.5 trust receipt that generated this memory.
